capabilities: Utilize OpenZeppelin's libraries for role management and apply SQL optimization strategies to ensure efficient and secure contract execution.

procedure.action: Install OpenZeppelin Contracts library using npm: `npm install @openzeppelin/contracts` using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Specify roles in Solidity using `AccessControl` from OpenZeppelin and assign them to functions by specifying schema, constraints, and invariants in machine-readable form; include at least one falsifiable acceptance criterion where error_rate < 0.001.

Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on SQL queries interacting with the contract to identify performance bottlenecks using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Refactor SQL queries to use indexed columns and optimize joins for improved execution time using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Release the smart contract and monitor role-based access logs for unauthorized access attempts using blue-green deployment strategy; run smoke_test() against new instance before switching traffic; assert smoke_test_pass_rate >= 1.0 before promoting.

summary: Integrate OpenZeppelin's multi-role access control with SQL optimization techniques to enhance security and performance in smart contract systems.
title: Implement Multi-Role Access Control with OpenZeppelin and SQL Optimization

failureConditions: Unauthorized access due to improper role assignment.

Performance degradation from unoptimized SQL queries; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.

Security vulnerabilities from incorrect contract deployment; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
successConditions: Check role assignments using OpenZeppelin's `hasRole` function; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Verify SQL query performance improvements with a 20% reduction in execution time; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Audit access logs for unauthorized access attempts post-deployment; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
